description:
src/lib.rs:155 builds the vault URL via format!("https://{vault_name}.vault.azure.net") without validating that vault_name only contains characters legal in an Azure Key Vault name (3-24 alphanumeric + hyphens). SecretUri::parse percent-decodes the path but does not enforce a charset.

Trigger: a secretx URI such as 'secretx:azure-kv:foo.example.com%23/secret' decodes vault_name to 'foo.example.com#'. Url::parse then sees 'https://foo.example.com#.vault.azure.net/secrets/secret' which is host 'foo.example.com' with fragment '.vault.azure.net'.

Bad behavior: the Azure SDK obtains an Azure-scope bearer token via ManagedIdentityCredential (or developer cred) and attaches it as 'Authorization: Bearer ...' to the request. The request is dispatched to the attacker-controlled host with a valid Azure token in the header. This is an Azure access-token exfiltration primitive whenever URI input is not fully trusted.

Fix: in from_uri, validate vault_name with a regex or explicit char check before constructing the URL. Reject anything that is not [A-Za-z0-9-]{3,24}. Return SecretError::InvalidUri with an actionable message.
title: vault_name not validated; allows attacker-controlled host injection
